roles.name: Python Developer
roles.description: Master the world's most popular language for AI, Data Science, and Web Dev.

lessons.title: Variables
lessons.explanation: Variables are used to store data. You can assign values using '='.

examples.title: Example
examples.code:
x = 10
y = 20
print(x + y)
examples.explanation: x and y store values. print() outputs their sum.

practice.problem: Create variables a=5 and b=7. Print their sum.
practice.starter_code: # Write your code here

lessons.title: Data Types
lessons.explanation: Python supports multiple data types like int, float, string, and boolean.

examples.title: Example
examples.code:
x = 10
y = 3.5
name = 'John'
print(type(x), type(name))
examples.explanation: type() shows the data type of variables.

practice.problem: Create a variable name='Alex' and print its type.
practice.starter_code: # Write your code here

lessons.title: Operators
lessons.explanation: Operators perform operations like addition, subtraction, multiplication.

examples.title: Example
examples.code:
a = 10
b = 3
print(a + b, a * b)

practice.problem: Multiply 6 and 7 and print the result.
practice.starter_code: # Write your code here

lessons.title: If-Else
lessons.explanation: Used to make decisions in code.

examples.title: Example

practice.problem: Check if a number is even or odd.
practice.starter_code:
num = 10
# Your code here

lessons.title: Loops
lessons.explanation: Loops repeat a block of code.

examples.title: Example
examples.code:
for i in range(3):
    print(i)

practice.problem: Print numbers from 1 to 5.
practice.starter_code: # Use a loop

lessons.title: Functions
lessons.explanation: Functions are reusable blocks of code.

examples.title: Example

practice.problem: Create a function to square a number.

lessons.title: Lists
lessons.explanation: Lists store multiple values.

examples.title: Example
examples.code:
nums = [1,2,3]
print(nums[0])

practice.problem: Create list [10,20,30] and print last element.
practice.starter_code:
nums = [10, 20, 30]
# Your code here

lessons.title: Dictionaries
lessons.explanation: Store data in key-value pairs.

examples.title: Example
examples.code:
person = {'name':'John','age':20}
print(person['name'])

practice.problem: Create dictionary with name and age and print age.
practice.starter_code:
person = {'name': 'Alex', 'age': 20}
# Your code here

lessons.title: OOP
lessons.explanation: Object-Oriented Programming uses classes and objects.

examples.title: Example

practice.problem: Create class Car with attribute brand.
practice.starter_code:
class Car:
    # Your code here

c = Car('Toyota')
print(c.brand)

lessons.title: File Handling
lessons.explanation: Used to read and write files.

examples.title: Example
examples.code:
f = open('test.txt','w')
f.write('Hello')
f.close()

practice.problem: Write 'Hi' into a file.
practice.starter_code:
# File handling practice
print('File created')

roles.name: Java Developer
roles.description: Build robust, scalable enterprise applications with Java.

lessons.title: Variables
lessons.explanation: Variables are used to store data values. In Java, you must declare the type of the variable.

examples.title: Example
examples.explanation: int defines integer variables. System.out.println prints output.

practice.problem: Create two integer variables a=5 and b=7. Print their sum.

lessons.explanation: Java supports primitive data types like int, double, char, and boolean.

examples.title: Example
examples.explanation: Different data types store different kinds of values.

lessons.explanation: Loops allow repeated execution of code.

lessons.title: Methods
lessons.explanation: Methods are functions in Java used to perform tasks.

lessons.explanation: Arrays store multiple values of same type.

lessons.explanation: Strings represent text in Java.

roles.name: C Developer
roles.description: Understand the foundations of computing with low-level C programming.

lessons.title: Variables
lessons.explanation: Variables are used to store data. In C, you must declare the type before using them.
keyPoints: Variables must be declared before use

examples.title: Example
examples.explanation: int declares integers. printf prints output using format specifier %d.

practice.problem: Create two integers a=3 and b=4. Print their sum.

lessons.explanation: C provides different data types to store different kinds of values.

examples.title: Example

lessons.explanation: Loops allow repeated execution of code blocks.

lessons.title: Functions
lessons.explanation: Functions are reusable blocks of code.
keyPoints: Must be defined before use or declared
